sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see a clearer pattern:
- After SPACE: The yellow cross split into 4 arms (11px each) with white center at (27, 21), and the blue cross disappeared
- After UP: The white center moved from (27, 21) to (24, 21) - moved UP by 3. The yellow cross arms also moved up (from rows 16-28 to rows 13-25).
So SPACE swaps control: before SPACE, arrow keys moved the blue cross. After SPACE, arrow keys move the YELLOW cross (which is now split into 4 arms with a white center).
Wait, let me re-examine. The yellow cross arms are at:
- (13, 21) - top arm (11px)
- (24, 10) - left arm (11px)
- (24, 22) - right arm (11px)
- (25, 21) - bottom arm (11px)
Center at (24, 21)
Originally the yellow cross was at rows 16-38, cols 10-32, centered at (27, 21). Now it's centered at (24, 21) - moved up by 3.
So SPACE toggles which cross is active. When yellow is active, it moves as a cross shape. The blue cross disappears when yellow is active.
Let me think about the goal:
- There are 4 yellow-centered squares and 4 blue-centered squares
- The yellow cross might need to collect the yellow squares
- The blue cross might need to collect the blue squares
- SPACE switches between controlling yellow and blue crosses
Let me verify by trying to move the yellow cross toward one of the yellow-centered squares. The yellow square at (17, 15) is closest. From center (24, 21), I need to go UP 7 and LEFT 6, which is about 3 UP and 2 LEFT steps.
Actually, let me first go back to the blue cross to understand both mechanics. Let me press SPACE again to switch back.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model updated: SPACE toggles which cross is active. Before SPACE, blue cross is controlled by arrows. After SPACE, yellow cross is controlled (blue disappears). Yellow cross moved UP 3 cells after pressing UP. The goal likely involves collecting same-colored squares with each cross.

sections.content:

<parameter=code>
# SPACE toggles control between blue and yellow crosses
# Currently yellow cross is active, center at (24, 21)
# Yellow-centered squares at: (3,15), (9,6), (9,24), (17,15)
# Blue-centered squares at: (16,48), (24,40), (24,53), (35,48)

# Let me try to collect a yellow square by moving toward it
# Yellow square at (17, 15) is closest to current center (24, 21)
# Need: UP 7, LEFT 6 ≈ 3 UP + 2 LEFT

# But first let me check: does the cross collect squares when it overlaps them?
# The cross arms extend 11px from center in each direction
# From (24, 21), the cross covers rows ~13-35, cols ~10-32
# Square at (17, 15) is within that range already!

# Let me try SPACE to switch back to blue, then try collecting blue squares
# Actually, let me first try to collect the yellow square at (17,15)
# Move LEFT to get closer
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
